questions.text: Who discovered cells for the first time in 1665?
questions.explanation: Hooke observed thin slices of cork under a self-made microscope and saw structures resembling honeycombs.

questions.text: Who discovered free-living cells in pond water for the first time?
questions.explanation: Leeuwenhoek used an improved microscope to see living cells.

questions.text: Nucleus in the cell was discovered by:
questions.explanation: Robert Brown discovered the nucleus in 1831.

questions.text: The term 'Protoplasm' for the fluid substance of the cell was coined by:
questions.explanation: J.E. Purkinje coined the term in 1839.

questions.text: Cell theory was proposed by:
questions.explanation: They stated that all plants and animals are composed of cells.

questions.text: Who expanded the cell theory by suggesting that all cells arise from pre-existing cells?
questions.explanation: Rudolf Virchow (1855) stated 'Omnis cellula e cellula'.

questions.text: Which of the following is a unicellular organism?
questions.explanation: Amoeba, Chlamydomonas, Paramoecium, and Bacteria are unicellular.

questions.text: The plasma membrane is made up of:
questions.explanation: It is a flexible organic bilayer mainly composed of lipids and proteins.

questions.text: Plasma membrane is called selectively permeable because it:
options: Allows some materials while preventing others
questions.explanation: It regulates the movement of substances in and out of the cell.

questions.text: Movement of water molecules through a selectively permeable membrane is called:
questions.explanation: Osmosis is a special case of diffusion involving water and a membrane.

questions.text: If a cell is placed in a solution with lower water concentration than the cell, it will:
questions.explanation: Water moves out of the cell in a hypertonic solution, causing it to shrink.

questions.text: The cell wall in plants is mainly composed of:
questions.explanation: Cellulose is a complex substance which provides structural strength to plants.

questions.text: Shrinkage of cell contents away from the cell wall when a plant cell loses water is:
questions.explanation: This occurs when a living plant cell is placed in a hypertonic solution.

questions.text: Which organelle contains genetic material (DNA)?
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains chromosomes, which are made of DNA and proteins.

questions.text: Functional segments of DNA are called:
questions.explanation: Genes carry hereditary information from parents to offspring.

questions.text: Organisms whose cells lack a nuclear membrane are called:
questions.explanation: Prokaryotic cells (like bacteria) have an undefined nuclear region called a nucleoid.

questions.text: Prokaryotic cells lack:
questions.explanation: They do not have mitochondria, plastids, or a defined nucleus.

questions.text: Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum (RER) looks rough because of the presence of:
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are attached to its surface and are sites of protein synthesis.

questions.text: Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (SER) helps in the manufacture of:
questions.explanation: SER is important for lipid synthesis and detoxification.

questions.text: Which organelle is involved in the formation of lysosomes?
questions.explanation: Golgi apparatus is involved in packaging and secretion.

questions.text: Lysosomes are known as:
questions.explanation: They contain digestive enzymes that can destroy the cell if it gets damaged.

questions.text: Which organelle is known as the 'Powerhouse of the cell'?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria generate energy in the form of ATP.

questions.text: ATP stands for:
questions.explanation: ATP is the energy currency of the cell.

questions.text: Which organelles have their own DNA and ribosomes?
questions.explanation: This allows them to make some of their own proteins.

questions.text: Plastids containing the pigment chlorophyll are called:
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts are essential for photosynthesis in plants.

questions.text: Primary function of leucoplasts is:
questions.explanation: They are colorless plastids used for storage.

questions.text: Animal cells usually have ________ vacuoles compared to plant cells.
questions.explanation: Plant cells often have a very large central vacuole (50-90% volume).

questions.text: Cell wall is ________ in animal cells.
questions.explanation: Animal cells only have a plasma membrane.

questions.text: Which process allows Amoeba to acquire its food?
questions.explanation: The flexibility of the cell membrane allows it to engulf food particles.

questions.text: The inner membrane of mitochondria is deeply folded to:
options: Increase surface area for ATP production
questions.explanation: These folds are called cristae.

questions.text: Who said 'All cells arise from pre-existing cells'?
questions.explanation: This completed the basic tenets of the cell theory.

questions.text: Chromosome number in a human cell is:
questions.explanation: Humans have 23 pairs, making 46 chromosomes in total.

questions.text: During cell division, chromatin material condenses to form:
questions.explanation: Chromosomes are rod-shaped structures visible only during division.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a membrane-bound organelle?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are found in both prokaryotes and eukaryotes and lack a membrane.

questions.text: Protein synthesis takes place in:
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are often called the 'Protein factories' of the cell.

questions.text: The liquid ground substance of the cell is:
questions.explanation: Cytoplasm is the region between the plasma membrane and the nucleus.

questions.text: Detoxification of poisons and drugs in liver cells is a function of:
questions.explanation: Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum plays a crucial role in detoxification.

questions.text: Plasmolysis in a plant cell is defined as:
options: Shrinkage of cytoplasm in hypertonic medium
questions.explanation: It's the loss of water through osmosis.

questions.text: Bacteria are ________ organisms.
questions.explanation: They lack a defined nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.

questions.text: Cell wall of fungi is made of:
questions.explanation: Chitin is a tough complex sugar.
